mnemonics.title: RGB Color Addition
mnemonics.content: R+R+G = Yellow (like a school bus), G+B = Cyan (sky blue), R+B = Magenta (flowers). Remember: ADD to get light - RGB makes WHITE!

mnemonics.title: CMY Subtraction
mnemonics.content: C+M = Blue, M+Y = Red, C+Y = Green. Remember: PRINT removes light - CMY makes BLACK (with K for rich black).

mnemonics.title: Keyframe Types
mnemonics.content: KFB: Keyframes Bind Frames - they BIND the animation path together. Think of them as anchors on a rope.

mnemonics.title: Animation Easing
mnemonics.content: Ease-IN: Starts slow (like a car accelerating). Ease-OUT: Ends slow (like a car braking). Ease-IN-OUT: Both - like a pendulum swing.

acronyms.usage: Additive color model for displays - combines light to create colors
Subtractive color model for printing - absorbs light to create colors
Intuitive color model based on human perception of color
RGB with alpha channel for transparency (0=transparent, 255=opaque)
Measures animation smoothness - higher fps = smoother motion
Color space used in video transmission for color compression

visualTips.visualization: Imagine three flashlights (Red, Green, Blue) overlapping on a white screen. Overlap areas create new colors - R+G=Yellow, G+B=Cyan, R+B=Magenta, all three=White.
Think of three 256-step staircases (one for R, one for G, one for B). Each step represents intensity from 0 (dark) to 255 (bright). Three staircases combine to make any color.
Think of keyframes as snapshots in a flipbook. The keyframes are pages you draw; tweening is the computer filling in all the pages between your snapshots.
Imagine looking through colored glass. Alpha=255 is opaque glass (no see-through). Alpha=0 is perfectly clear (invisible). Alpha=128 is frosted glass (50% see-through).

analogies.analogy: Additive (RGB) is like a darkroom - you START with darkness (black) and ADD light to brighten. Subtractive (CMY) is like painting - you START with white (paper) and ADD pigment to darken.
Frame rate is like page-flipping speed in a flipbook. 24 fps means flipping 24 pages each second - fast enough for your brain to see continuous motion instead of separate pages.
Tweening is like an auto-complete for animation. If you type 'univer' and auto-complete gives 'university', tweening is the computer completing the animation between your keyframes.

Color depth is like the number of paint colors available. 1-bit (2 colors) is only black and white - like coloring with just two crayons. 24-bit is having 16.7 million colors - like having every color imaginable.
